title: architecture.yaml — architecture-as-code produced by /architect (refactor-003 + feat-002)
description: Written by the architect agent (task 020) post-design-signoff, consumed by PM (task 021), skills-audit --scope=build (task 038), and all builders (028/029/030). Refactor-002/003 fixed the ordering; feat-002 (stack-skill-shelf) introduced `tooling.stack` as the stack-dispatch slot: per-tier stack-slug values that resolve to .claude/skills/agents/{tier}/{stack-slug}/SKILL.md. Builders use tooling.stack to load the right per-stack prompt pack at run time. `additionalProperties: false` only on the stack block (architecture.yaml itself allows consumer-specific fields; stack is locked).

apps.description: Per-app configuration. Keys are app names (web, mobile, api, admin, etc.). Each entry's `framework` field is a FREE-TEXT mirror of the corresponding tooling.stack slot (for human readability) — the authoritative stack choice lives in tooling.stack.

packages.description: Shared packages (ui-kit, types, utils, api-client, orchestrator-contracts).

icon_library.description: Mirrored from docs/selected-style.json.iconLibrary — NOT decided here (refactor-003).

design_dials.description: Mirrored from docs/selected-style.json.dials — NOT decided here.

mcp_servers.description: Design-stage servers pre-registered at /new-project time. Architect appends build-stage additions (usually zero — vendor SDKs are NPM, not MCP).

stackRationale.description: Architect's justification for each stack pick (one entry per tooling.stack slot). Refactor-002/feat-002: surfaces the reasoning to the PM + builders so downstream agents understand WHY a stack was chosen. Human readable + audit trail.

slot.description: Which tooling.stack field this rationale explains. Example: 'web_framework', 'backend_framework'.

pick.description: The stack-slug chosen for this slot (e.g. 'react-next', 'python-fastapi').

reason.description: Short prose justification. Should reference brief §7/§8 signals, competitor stacks from docs/analysis/shared/competitors.md, or integrations-options.md signals.

briefSignal.description: Quote or reference into brief.md that influenced this pick, or null if architect-inferred.

rejected.description: Other stack-slugs the architect considered and rejected for this slot.

stack.description: Stack slots. Each value is a stack-slug that matches a directory under .claude/skills/agents/{tier}/. null means 'no app of that tier' (e.g., mobile-less projects set mobile_framework: null).

web_framework.description: Web app framework. null if the project has no web tier. Value resolves to .claude/skills/agents/front-end/{value}/SKILL.md. Initial shipped stacks: react-next, svelte-kit. Others are reserved for future stack-skill authoring via /skills-audit --scope=build.

web_styling.description: Web styling approach. Tailwind is the factory default; other values trigger stack-skill alternate patterns.

mobile_framework.description: Mobile framework. null if no mobile tier. Initial shipped: expo-rn.

backend_language.description: Backend runtime language. Paired with backend_framework.

backend_framework.description: Backend framework. null if no backend tier. Initial shipped: node-trpc-nest, python-fastapi.

orm.description: ORM / query builder. null if backend uses no ORM (raw SQL / NoSQL-only).

database.description: Primary data store. Informational — drives Docker Compose + dev-setup generation more than builder prompt composition.

persistence_layer.description: feat-038 Phase 2B — drives the E2E data-seeding strategy per `.claude/rules/testing-policy.md §E2E data-seeding strategy`. localStorage → Strategy A (per-test reseed via localStorage.clear). external-api-only → Strategy D (page.route() interception of upstream API). real-db → Strategy C (hybrid: globalSetup baseline + describe-block scoped /test/seed). null/absent: synthesizer infers from web_framework/backend_framework/database fields (database != null → real-db; database null + backend_framework non-null → external-api-only; both null → localStorage). Architects MUST set this explicitly when the project has a non-trivial persistence layer; the inference is a backstop for legacy manifests.
